pi_items.source: QBlade v0.96 sample projects directory (not accessible without installation)
Task description and setup/export_result.sh scripts confirm this path
Task description and setup/export_result.sh scripts confirm this path
12 m/s is a standard rated power wind speed for HAWTs per wind energy literature; typical rated wind speeds are 10-15 m/s
TSR of 7 is below the typical optimum of 8-10 but is within reasonable operating range for wind turbines
QBlade documentation confirms 'Single Point' is a valid simulation type in the Turbine Simulation module

QBlade outputs normal force in N/m (Newtons per meter), NOT N (Newtons), per multiple search results confirming 'normal force is always defined per length unit'. The metadata key uses '_n' suffix which stands for 'normal force', and the value of 100 N/m is within reasonable range for blade loads.
Spanwise loads export typically includes: Position, Normal Force, Tangential Force = 3 columns, which is consistent with QBlade aerodynamic blade graph data structure

privileged_info_summary: The task uses QBlade v0.96 to perform steady-state BEM (Blade Element Momentum) simulations with wind speed of 12 m/s (typical rated power condition for HAWTs) and a Tip Speed Ratio of 7 (below but near optimal range). QBlade outputs normal force in units of N/m (Newtons per meter of blade span), and spanwise load exports contain Position, Normal Force, and Tangential Force as three columns. The sample project 'Turbine Simulation.wpa' is a QBlade project file that should be available in the v0.96 sample_projects directory, though it could not be independently verified without access to an installed QBlade instance.
